page_content:
SPAN461 Queer Spain (3 Credits) Explores discursive representations of Spaniards who were judged as non-normative by their society during the modern and contemporary period. Putting a critical lens to various cultural artifacts (visual arts, ﬁction, ﬁlm, and journalism), we will interrogate what it means to be "(ab)normal" in terms of gender expression, erotic desire, sexuality, and anatomical sex in Spain at critical junctures in modern history. Taught in Spanish. Prerequisite: One of the following: SPAN331, SPAN332, SPAN333, SPAN361, SPAN362 or SPAN363; or permission of instructor. Recommended: SPAN333.

SPAN462 Twentieth Century Drama (3 Credits) Signiﬁcant plays of the twentieth century. Prerequisite: SPAN333, SPAN361, SPAN331, SPAN332, SPAN362, or SPAN363.

SPAN463 Cultural Artifacts of Contemporary Spanish Crises from 1898 to the COVID Pandemic (3 Credits) An overview of Spain from the end of the 19th century through the present day and studies some of its main crises. The course explores the production of literary, cultural, and cinematic texts in their sociohistorical, political, religious and cultural contexts and development. This interdisciplinary course covers topics such as political, historical, religious, racial, ethnic, gendered/sexual, cultural, and ethno-geographical, literary and cinematic diversities and differences in Contemporary Spain. Taught in Spanish. Prerequisite: SPAN331, SPAN332, SPAN333, SPAN361, SPAN362, or SPAN363; or permission of instructor.
